article_title: LEO Broadband Race Heats Up as Amazon Seeks Deadline Extension and Rivals Scale Military and Enterprise Offerings

content_preview.title: LEO Broadband Race Heats Up as Amazon Seeks Deadline Extension and Rivals Scale Military and Enterprise Offerings
content_preview.summary: Amazon has asked the FCC for a two-year extension on its satellite deployment deadline, citing a rocket shortage, while Eutelsat orders 340 new OneWeb satellites and Telesat adds military Ka-band to Lightspeed as competitors carve out niches against Starlink's 10-million-subscriber lead.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The low-Earth orbit satellite broadband market is entering a new phase of competition in early 2026, shaped less by raw launch counts than by regulatory deadlines, strategic pivots, and differentiated hardware. Amazon filed with the Federal Communications Commission in January for a two-year extension on its obligation to deploy half of its 3,232-satellite constellation by July 2026, disclosing that it expects to have only about 700 satellites in orbit by that deadline. editor_notes:
Comprehensive Analysis piece covering the LEO broadband competitive landscape with strong multi-source reporting. Summary accurately reflects content. All 6 sources verified. Word count (1123) appropriate for Analysis. Note: the 975 million euros financing figure is not confirmed by the Airbus press release directly but may come from supplementary Eutelsat filings.

source_verification:
- spacenews.com (Amazon terminal): CONFIRMED. Leo Ultra 1 Gbps down/400 Mbps up, full-duplex phased-array, no moving parts, fastest commercial phased-array in production, Leo Pro enterprise.
- spacenews.com (Telesat): CONFIRMED. December 2026 pathfinder launch, MDA Space builder, 750 kg satellites, 14 Falcon 9 launches, early 2028 full service.
- airbus.com: CONFIRMED. 340 additional OneWeb satellites, Airbus Defence and Space, initial 100 order Dec 2024, Toulouse facility, digital channelizers, deliveries from end 2026.
- globenewswire.com (Telesat): CONFIRMED. 500 MHz military Ka-band, 156 satellites, NATO/allied defense demand, adjacent to commercial Ka-band.
- aviationweek.com: CONFIRMED. Amazon rebranded to Amazon Leo November 2025, 150+ satellites in orbit, 3200+ planned, commercial service targeting multiple countries.
- aboutamazon.com: CONFIRMED. Direct-to-AWS links, Leo Ultra/Pro terminals, routing without public internet, enterprise preview customers.
